meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn to implement SaaS pricing and overcome version control issues. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your app.
content.answer_block: Implement a robust versioning strategy by linking your SaaS pricing logic to deployment snapshots. Use Antigravity to automate branch-based environments and Claude Code to enforce schema validation. This ensures that every pricing tier update is version-controlled, testable, and safely deployed, preventing production drift and data loss.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen of broken code, realizing the 'final_v2_real' folder you created is now a graveyard of overwritten logic. The panic is real; without version control, your first SaaS project is a house of cards waiting for the slightest breeze of a git-merge error to collapse. You need a system that treats your code as a professional asset, not a collection of zip files.

But here is the secret: version control isn't just about saving files—it's about building a scalable business architecture. By integrating your SaaS pricing logic into a versioned deployment pipeline, you turn a chaotic hobby project into a professional, revenue-generating engine. Let’s stop the manual file-swapping and start building like a senior architect.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Architecture
steps.description: Establish a Git-based foundation that separates your pricing logic from UI components to prevent accidental overwrites.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a directory structure for a SaaS app that separates pricing schema, payment gateway logic, and frontend UI, ensuring modularity.

steps.title: Define Pricing Schema Versioning
steps.description: Create a version-controlled JSON schema for your pricing tiers so every change is tracked and revertible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a versioned JSON schema for three SaaS pricing tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise) with fields for features, limits, and price.

steps.title: Automate Pricing Logic Deployment
steps.description: Use Claude Code to create a deployment script that validates the pricing schema before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a validation script that checks the pricing JSON against the schema before deployment to ensure no breaking changes occur.

steps.title: Implement Feature Flagging
steps.description: Decouple pricing updates from code releases, allowing you to roll back pricing changes instantly without a full redeploy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature flag management module that toggles pricing tiers based on environment variables.

steps.title: Finalize CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Configure an automated pipeline that runs your test suite every time a pricing change is committed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit tests on pricing logic whenever a new tag is pushed to the repo.

pro_tips: Always commit your pricing logic as a separate migration file to keep your history clean.
Use semantic versioning (SemVer) for your pricing tiers; never overwrite a 'v1' tier directly.
Treat environment variables as code; store them in a secure, encrypted secrets manager rather than local files.
Implement an 'audit log' table in your database that records which version of the pricing schema was active for every user transaction.
Practice 'Git flow' early; keep your main branch pristine and do all experiments in feature branches.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Now that your pricing logic is version-controlled, focus on integrating a payment provider like Stripe to connect your schema to real revenue.
content.conversion_block: You've moved from disorganized file-swapping to professional-grade version control. This is the exact workflow used by top-tier SaaS engineers to maintain stability while scaling revenue. Don't let your code base become a bottleneck. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
